summarization.a: The figure shows a XRD diffractograms with peaks at various 2Theta angles for CoO samples exposed to different doses of O3: no exposure, 2 doses, 10 doses, and 410 doses.

data_extraction.a:
| Intensity (arb. units) | Initial | 2 doses of O3 | 10 doses of O3 | 410 doses of O3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 20 | - | - | peak at 19° | peak at 19° |
| 25 | - | - | - | - |
| 30 | peak at 31°, 34° | peak at 31°, 34° | peak at 31°, 34° | peak at 31° |
| 35 | peak at 36° | peak at 36° | peak at 36° | peak at 36° |
| 40 | - | peak at 43° | peak at 43° | peak at 43° |
| 45 | peak at 47° | - | peak at 45° | peak at 45° |
| 50 | - | - | - | - |
| 55 | peak at 57° | - | - | - |

a.question: Which crystal planes can be seen in the diagram?
Which species can be observed on the initial surface, and after 2 doses of O3?
a.answer: Initially, only h-CoO can be observed on the surface. After 2 doses od ozone, both h-CoO and c-CoO can be seen.

a.question: Which crystal planes can be observed after 2 doses of O3?
a.answer: The (100), (002), and (101) correspond to the h-CoO, while the (200) crystal plane is related to the c-CoO.

a.question: What effect does the O3 dosing have on the oxidation of the CoO?
a.answer: As the number of O3 doses increases, the CoO turns into c-Co3O4.
